phase_name: M1 Phase 2 (Controller Loop) — context locked, ready for plan + execute

PARITY-04 /tf + /tf_static close via rclpy parity publishers

VERIFICATION.md flipped to gaps_resolved; CLAUDE.md venv-activate req added
D-04 empirically disproven; cable wedge no longer reproduces under current launch; CLAUDE.md cable note revised; probe_cable_wedge.py + probe_cable_behavior.py added

Phase 1 stale-flag cleanup: 8 flags (PARITY-01/02/05, TEX-01/02, DX-02/03/04) flipped to [x] with closure citations; DX-03/DX-04 traceability phase mappings corrected to Phase 1; STATE.md status flipped to between-phases

/gsd-discuss-phase 2 --auto: 02-CONTEXT.md (13 decisions D-01..D-13) + 02-DISCUSSION-LOG.md (12 areas). Architecture locked: AicControllerLoop class mirrors AicParityPublishers inverted; physics-tick rclpy node owns 2 subs (joint_commands, pose_commands) + 2 pubs (controller_state, off_limit Contacts); Lula RMPflow pose IK; omni.physx contact-report drives off-limit; PYTHONPATH-link to ~/Documents/aic/.pixi/envs/default for aic_control_interfaces

remaining_tasks.name: /gsd-plan-phase 2 --auto — chain spawns: gsd-phase-researcher (read CONTEXT.md, produce 02-RESEARCH.md), gsd-pattern-mapper (produce 02-PATTERNS.md from CONTEXT+RESEARCH file lists), gsd-planner (produce 02-XX-PLAN.md set), gsd-plan-checker (verify quality, revise loop max 3). All 13 CONTEXT.md decisions are LOCKED — researcher should not re-research them, planner should not re-decide them. Suggest --skip-ui flag (Phase 2 has only 2 mechanical UI buttons per DX-02 4-surface contract; UI-SPEC.md is bureaucratic overhead for that scope).
/gsd-execute-phase 2 --auto — wave-based parallel execution per plan dependencies; gsd-executor agents one per plan; atomic commits per plan

Build & run smoke_test_aic_controller.py (per CONTEXT D-12) — 7-step Python 3.10 rclpy verifier mirroring smoke_test_aic_parity.py structure: connect → publish JointMotionUpdate → verify articulation moved → publish MotionUpdate → verify EE moved → verify ControllerState non-zero → drive into off-limit → verify Contacts non-empty. N/N pass = closure.
Phase 2 closure: 02-SUMMARY.md + flip PARITY-06/09/10/11 to [x] in REQUIREMENTS.md + STATE.md status to between-phases. Mirror Phase 1 closure pattern.

/gsd-discuss-phase 3 --auto — Cable Physics + Object TF (SCENE-02/03/05/06, PARITY-07/08). SCENE-05 is mechanical USD mass/inertia edit per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template (~3-4 hrs), NOT research-gated.
Phase 4: trial loader + end-to-end M1 verification under live aic_engine + CheatCode (DX-04 final + DX-05 + the actual 'every trial passes' bar)
M1 deliverable: every sample_config.yaml trial passes under CheatCode against Isaac Sim with the same per-trial outcomes Gazebo produces

human_actions_pending.reason: Strategic pause point per HANDOFF.json autonomous-mode 'pause on context exhaustion' clause. Plan + execute chain for Phase 2 needs a fresh context window — spawning 6+ subagents (researcher + pattern-mapper + planner + checker + N executors) consumes substantial budget; current session is at ~140K used (workflow reads + Phase 1 artifacts + Phase 2 CONTEXT writing). Rather than risk context exhaustion mid-execution, this checkpoint lets the next session boot with full HANDOFF continuity and a fresh budget.

decisions.decision: Lock in autonomous M1 execution. No questions to user until milestone deliverable produced. User's only intervention: pause/resume on context exhaustion.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly authorized; Phase 1 closure pattern (this session) is the template; all major architectural decisions surfaced and addressed; defaults documented for the few autonomous calls (cable physics, IK library, trial-pass tolerance, Docker autonomy).

decisions.decision: Pause-resume cadence: each major workflow chunk (discuss, plan, execute, smoke, close) gets its own /clear-friendly checkpoint with HANDOFF.json updated between them. Discuss-phase + Plan-phase fit in one session; Plan-phase + Execute-phase do NOT (too many subagents).
decisions.rationale: Context-discipline lesson from this session: reading the plan-phase workflow (~80K tokens) + writing 13-decision CONTEXT.md + spawning agents would exceed budget if all chained in one session. Phase 1 closure proved the pattern: one clean checkpoint per major workflow chunk.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 architecture (locked in 02-CONTEXT.md D-01..D-13): AicControllerLoop class mirrors AicParityPublishers inverted — physics-tick rclpy node owns 2 subs (joint_commands, pose_commands) + 2 pubs (controller_state, off_limit Contacts). Lula RMPflow pose IK with PyKDL fallback. omni.physx contact-report for off-limit (NOT broken Isaac Sim 5.0 ContactSensor). PYTHONPATH-link to ~/Documents/aic/.pixi/envs/default for aic_control_interfaces. 2 MCP atoms (setup_controller_subscribers + setup_offlimit_contacts) per DX-02 4-surface contract. Per-joint impedance applied to articulation drives; Cartesian impedance fields ignored as controller-side math.
decisions.rationale: All gray areas resolved during /gsd-discuss-phase 2 --auto with HANDOFF.json D-2/D-3 locks honored + robot-collision-forensics skill consulted for off-limit + Phase 1 D-10/D-11 architectural precedent extended.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 plan-phase invocation should include --skip-ui flag.
decisions.rationale: Phase 2 adds 2 UI buttons per DX-02 4-surface contract — that's mechanical, not design. UI-SPEC.md generation is bureaucratic overhead for a button-add. The workflow's UI gate triggers because ROADMAP Phase 2 text contains 'aic_control_interfaces' which matches its 'interface' grep — false positive.

decisions.decision: SCENE-05 cable physics strategy = mechanical USD mass/inertia edit (D-06 in-place USD edit policy) per NVIDIA RigidBodyRopeDemo.py template — NOT research-gated cable-physics-strategy decision
decisions.rationale: Empirical re-test 2026-05-03 disproved D-04: with cable SetActive(True) and current launch path (postload + warm cache + venv-activate), play_scene returns instantly, sim time advances at 0.67x realtime, no main-thread block, MCP latency <30ms. Cable wedge no longer reproduces. Remaining issue is mass=0/inertia=0 → PhysX skips the bodies (decorative only). Solution: author proper mass/inertia per rope-demo template (~3-4 hrs).

decisions.decision: Cable physics fallback hierarchy: (1) author mass/inertia in-place; (2) if wedge returns under non-zero mass, investigate aic-dt extension Kit env-specific init order; (3) re-author rope using NVIDIA's PhysxPhysicsJointInstancer template; (4) hybrid rigid-plug-with-visual-cable as deepest fallback. Skip deformable bodies entirely.
decisions.rationale: Bisection from prior session 29ca157f showed cable plays fine in bare Kit. Three approaches tried + failed: runtime override, bake-before-load, and old D-04 workaround. NVIDIA's rope demo is the validated template never tried. Hybrid is sufficient for CheatCode (only plug pose matters for trial pass/fail).

decisions.decision: IK library for PARITY-10 (/aic_controller/pose_commands) = Lula RMPflow first, fall back to PyKDL. Skip cuMotion as overkill.
decisions.rationale: aic_controller does the impedance math; Isaac Sim just applies resolved motion. Simplest IK that works.

decisions.decision: Phase 2 controller wiring = direct articulation drive from rclpy subscriber, no ros2_control plugin layer. Mirror Phase 1's rclpy publisher pattern but inverted (sink, not source).
decisions.rationale: aic_controller IS the controller; Isaac Sim is the 'hardware' sink. Don't double up the controller layer.

decisions.decision: Trial-pass tolerance = iterate until 20/20 sample_config.yaml trials pass. Don't ship M1 with partial passes.
decisions.rationale: Core Value bar is 'every trial passes' — partial-pass is not M1.

decisions.decision: Docker autonomy = OK to bring up aic_eval + aic_model containers via docker run when Phase 4 verification needs them. Tear down after each round.
decisions.rationale: Per CLAUDE.md the Docker bringup is at ~/Documents/aic/scripts/run_cheatcode.sh; standard docker run, no system service modification.

decisions.decision: Resume etiquette: pick up from HANDOFF.json with no preamble; just continue.
decisions.rationale: User explicitly requested silent autonomous execution.

next_action: On resume: verify infrastructure (Isaac Sim still RUNNING + RESPONSIVE on 8768; ROS humble env clean; DerivedDataCache ≥100MB; venv-activate sourced if Isaac Sim restarted). Then run `/gsd-plan-phase 2 --auto --skip-ui` (the --skip-ui flag suppresses bureaucratic UI-SPEC generation for the 2-mechanical-button scope per the locked decision in this handoff). The chain auto-advances: research → pattern-map → plan → check → execute. Expect 4-6 plans in 1-2 waves based on Phase 1 pattern. After execute returns PHASE COMPLETE, run smoke_test_aic_controller.py per CONTEXT D-12, then write 02-SUMMARY.md + flip PARITY-06/09/10/11 in REQUIREMENTS.md (mirror 01-SUMMARY.md + Phase 1 flag-cleanup pattern).
context_notes: Mental state at pause: Phase 2 fully scoped. 02-CONTEXT.md is the contract — researcher + planner + executor agents read it, do NOT re-decide its 13 locked decisions. Architecture is purely 'Phase 1 inverted' — same physics-tick rclpy class pattern, same venv-activate + LD discipline, same MCP 4-surface contract. Custom AIC message types loaded via PYTHONPATH-link to ~/Documents/aic/.pixi/envs/default (verified 3 .msg files exist: JointMotionUpdate, MotionUpdate, ControllerState). Off-limit contacts use omni.physx contact-report per robot-collision-forensics skill (NOT ContactSensor — broken in Isaac Sim 5.0). Lula RMPflow for pose IK (PyKDL fallback). 2 atoms total (setup_controller_subscribers + setup_offlimit_contacts) — much smaller surface than Phase 1's 9 plans. Probably 4-6 plans total (1-2 setup + 1 controller_loop module + 1-2 off-limit module + 1 quick_start integration + 1 smoke test). Per-joint impedance fields applied to articulation drives via set_gains(); Cartesian impedance is controller-side math, log + ignore. ControllerState publishes measured-fields (FK from Articulation) + echoes reference fields. drop-silently failure mode for bad commands. Live aic_eval container snapshot drives the off-limit prim filter set (mirror Phase 1 D-01 empirical principle). Phase 3 surprise (D-04 stale) collapsed SCENE-05 to ~3-4hr mechanical USD edit — Phase 3 is now small. Total M1 estimate revised down: Phase 2 (~6-10 hrs autonomous) + Phase 3 (~3-4 hrs cable + ~2 hrs object TF) + Phase 4 (~12-16 hrs trial loader + Docker E2E) = ~25-32 hrs total. Smaller than original ~28-33 hr estimate.
